# ANSI Display Support Matrix

<!-- This file is generated by scripts/generate-ansi-display-support-report.mjs. -->

This report is generated from `src/core/terminal/conformance/ansi-sequence-matrix.ts` and reflects the current display-facing ANSI/VT support ledger used by the conformance suite.

## Summary

- Total tracked display-facing families: 38
- Oracle Backed: 23
- State Backed: 6
- Host Facing: 2
- Deferred: 7

Status meanings:

- `oracle-backed`: Verified against xterm-headless fixtures and visible browser playback.
- `state-backed`: Implemented in parser/buffer state, but still missing some oracle or browser coverage.
- `host-facing`: Modeled by the parser/runtime, with final visible behavior owned by the host surface.
- `deferred`: Tracked intentionally, but not implemented faithfully yet.
- `rejected`: Explicitly out of scope for the current display-facing terminal surface.

## Oracle Backed

Verified against xterm-headless fixtures and visible browser playback.

| Family | Sequences | Coverage Source | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <code>c0-line-feed</code> | <code>\n</code> | oracle-backed via <code>c0-line-feed</code> | LF advances to the next row and participates in scrolling behavior. |
| <code>c0-carriage-return</code> | <code>\r</code> | oracle-backed via <code>c0-carriage-return</code> | CR returns the cursor to column 1 on the active row. |
| <code>c0-backspace</code> | <code>\b</code> | oracle-backed via <code>c0-backspace</code> | BS moves the cursor left without erasing the existing cell. |
| <code>c0-tab</code> | <code>\t</code> | oracle-backed via <code>c0-tab</code> | HT moves the cursor to the next active tab stop. |
| <code>csi-insert-chars</code> | <code>\u001b[2@</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-insert-chars</code> | CSI @ / ICH inserts blank cells at the cursor position. |
| <code>csi-cursor-relative</code> | <code>\u001b[3A</code>, <code>\u001b[4B</code>, <code>\u001b[5C</code>, <code>\u001b[6D</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-cursor-up</code>, <code>csi-cursor-down</code>, <code>csi-cursor-forward</code>, <code>csi-cursor-backward</code> | CSI A/B/C/D performs relative cursor motion. |
| <code>csi-cursor-position</code> | <code>\u001b[7;8H</code>, <code>\u001b[2;3f</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-cursor-position-h</code>, <code>csi-cursor-position-f</code> | CSI H/f performs row and column cursor positioning. |
| <code>csi-cha</code> | <code>\u001b[12G</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-cha</code> | CSI G / CHA sets an absolute column within the current row. |
| <code>csi-vpa</code> | <code>\u001b[4d</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-vpa</code> | CSI d / VPA sets an absolute row while preserving the current column. |
| <code>csi-cnl</code> | <code>\u001b[2E</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-cnl</code> | CSI E / CNL moves to a later row and returns to column 1. |
| <code>csi-cpl</code> | <code>\u001b[2F</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-cpl</code> | CSI F / CPL moves to an earlier row and returns to column 1. |
| <code>csi-insert-lines</code> | <code>\u001b[2L</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-insert-lines</code> | CSI L / IL inserts lines within the active scroll region. |
| <code>csi-delete-lines</code> | <code>\u001b[2M</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-delete-lines</code> | CSI M / DL deletes lines within the active scroll region. |
| <code>csi-delete-chars</code> | <code>\u001b[2P</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-delete-chars</code> | CSI P / DCH deletes characters from the cursor onward. |
| <code>csi-ech</code> | <code>\u001b[3X</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-ech</code> | CSI X / ECH erases character cells without moving the cursor. |
| <code>csi-rep</code> | <code>\u001b[5b</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-rep</code> | CSI b / REP repeats the preceding printable character. |
| <code>csi-scroll</code> | <code>\u001b[2S</code>, <code>\u001b[2T</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-scroll-up</code>, <code>csi-scroll-down</code> | CSI S/T scroll the active region up or down. |
| <code>csi-scroll-region</code> | <code>\u001b[2;4r</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-scroll-region</code> | CSI r defines the active top and bottom margins. |
| <code>csi-save-restore-cursor</code> | <code>\u001b[s</code>, <code>\u001b[u</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-save-cursor</code>, <code>csi-restore-cursor</code> | CSI s/u saves and restores the cursor position. |
| <code>csi-wraparound-mode</code> | <code>\u001b[?7h</code>, <code>\u001b[?7l</code> | oracle-backed via <code>csi-decset-wraparound-mode</code>, <code>csi-decrst-wraparound-mode</code> | CSI ?7h/l toggles wraparound behavior at the last column. |
| <code>esc-dec-save-restore-cursor</code> | <code>\u001b7</code>, <code>\u001b8</code> | oracle-backed via <code>esc-dec-save-cursor</code>, <code>esc-dec-restore-cursor</code> | ESC 7/8 saves and restores the cursor using DEC semantics. |
| <code>esc-index-next-line-reverse-index</code> | <code>\u001bD</code>, <code>\u001bE</code>, <code>\u001bM</code> | oracle-backed via <code>esc-index</code>, <code>esc-next-line</code>, <code>esc-reverse-index</code> | ESC D/E/M perform IND, NEL, and RI display motion semantics. |
| <code>esc-reset-to-initial-state</code> | <code>\u001bc</code> | oracle-backed via <code>esc-reset-to-initial-state</code> | ESC c / RIS resets the visible terminal state to its initial state. |

## State Backed

Implemented in parser/buffer state, but still missing some oracle or browser coverage.

| Family | Sequences | Coverage Source | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <code>c0-form-feed</code> | <code>\f</code> | parser-only via <code>c0-form-feed</code> | Parser coverage exists today, but the family still needs oracle-backed fixture coverage. |
| <code>csi-erase-display</code> | <code>\u001b[J</code>, <code>\u001b[2J</code>, <code>\u001b[3J</code> | oracle-backed, parser-only via <code>csi-erase-display-default</code>, <code>csi-erase-display-all</code>, <code>csi-erase-display-scrollback</code> | Modes 0 and 2 are oracle-backed today; 3J is parser-backed and still needs explicit display-state coverage. |
| <code>csi-erase-line</code> | <code>\u001b[K</code>, <code>\u001b[1K</code>, <code>\u001b[2K</code> | oracle-backed, parser-only via <code>csi-erase-line-default</code>, <code>csi-erase-line-start</code>, <code>csi-erase-line-all</code> | Only mode 0 is oracle-backed today; modes 1 and 2 are parser-backed and still need dedicated fixtures. |
| <code>sgr-visible-styling</code> | <code>\u001b[m</code>, <code>\u001b[1;2;5;7;8m</code>, <code>\u001b[31;44;91;102m</code>, <code>\u001b[38;5;196;48;5;25m</code>, <code>\u001b[38;2;17;34;51;48;2;68;85;102m</code> | parser-only, oracle-backed via <code>sgr-reset-default</code>, <code>sgr-attributes</code>, <code>sgr-ansi-16-colors</code>, <code>sgr-indexed-256-colors</code>, <code>sgr-truecolor</code> | The visible styling families are mostly oracle-backed; bare CSI m reset still only has parser-level coverage. |
| <code>csi-insert-mode</code> | <code>\u001b[4h</code>, <code>\u001b[4l</code> | oracle-backed, parser-only via <code>csi-set-insert-mode</code>, <code>csi-reset-insert-mode</code> | Insert-mode entry is oracle-backed today; the reset path still needs symmetric fixture coverage. |
| <code>csi-origin-mode</code> | <code>\u001b[?6h</code>, <code>\u001b[?6l</code> | oracle-backed, parser-only via <code>csi-decset-origin-mode</code>, <code>csi-decrst-origin-mode</code> | Origin-mode enablement is oracle-backed; the reset path still needs dedicated fixture coverage. |

## Host Facing

Modeled by the parser/runtime, with final visible behavior owned by the host surface.

| Family | Sequences | Coverage Source | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <code>csi-cursor-visibility</code> | <code>\u001b[?25h</code>, <code>\u001b[?25l</code> | host-facing via <code>csi-decset-cursor-visibility</code>, <code>csi-decrst-cursor-visibility</code> | CSI ?25h/l toggles whether the cursor is visibly rendered. |
| <code>csi-alternate-screen</code> | <code>\u001b[?47h</code>, <code>\u001b[?1047h</code>, <code>\u001b[?1049h</code>, <code>\u001b[?1049l</code> | host-facing via <code>csi-decset-alternate-screen-47</code>, <code>csi-decset-alternate-screen-1047</code>, <code>csi-decset-alternate-screen-1049</code>, <code>csi-decrst-alternate-screen-1049</code> | The parser models the visible state requests, but host-side surface switching still owns the final rendered behavior. |

## Deferred

Tracked intentionally, but not implemented faithfully yet.

| Family | Sequences | Coverage Source | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <code>csi-hpa</code> | <code>\u001b[10`</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>csi-hpa</code> | CSI ` / HPA sets an absolute horizontal position in the current row. |
| <code>csi-hpr</code> | <code>\u001b[5a</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>csi-hpr</code> | CSI a / HPR moves horizontally relative to the current cursor position. |
| <code>csi-vpr</code> | <code>\u001b[3e</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>csi-vpr</code> | CSI e / VPR moves vertically relative to the current cursor position. |
| <code>csi-unmodeled-dec-private-display-modes</code> | <code>\u001b[?5h</code>, <code>\u001b[?45h</code>, <code>\u001b[?1048h</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>unmodeled-dec-private-display-modes</code> | Additional display-facing DEC private modes remain unimplemented. |
| <code>tab-stop-management</code> | <code>\u001bH</code>, <code>\u001b[g</code>, <code>\u001b[3g</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>tab-stop-management</code> | ESC H and CSI g manage user-defined tab stops. |
| <code>charset-designation-and-locking-shifts</code> | <code>\u001b(B</code>, <code>\u001b(0</code>, <code>\u000e</code>, <code>\u000f</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>charset-designation-and-locking-shifts</code> | Charset designation, line-drawing alternates, and locking shifts affect visible glyph projection. |
| <code>c1-eight-bit-controls</code> | <code>\u009b31m</code>, <code>\u009b12G</code> | deferred gap ledger via <code>c1-eight-bit-controls</code> | 8-bit C1 variants should alias the same display-facing semantics as their 7-bit escape forms. |

## Deferred Follow-Up Ledger

These families remain intentionally open and should be treated as the next named fidelity backlog.

| Family | Deferred Sequences | Gap Ledger |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `csi-hpa` | <code>\u001b[10`</code> | <code>csi-hpa</code>: CSI ` / HPA horizontal position absolute is not implemented yet. |
| `csi-hpr` | <code>\u001b[5a</code> | <code>csi-hpr</code>: CSI a / HPR horizontal position relative is not implemented yet. |
| `csi-vpr` | <code>\u001b[3e</code> | <code>csi-vpr</code>: CSI e / VPR vertical position relative is not implemented yet. |
| `csi-unmodeled-dec-private-display-modes` | <code>\u001b[?5h</code>, <code>\u001b[?45h</code>, <code>\u001b[?1048h</code> | <code>unmodeled-dec-private-display-modes</code>: Only a targeted subset of display-facing DEC private modes is implemented; visual modes like reverse-video, reverse-wrap, and DEC save/restore variants remain future conformance work. |
| `tab-stop-management` | <code>\u001bH</code>, <code>\u001b[g</code>, <code>\u001b[3g</code> | <code>tab-stop-management</code>: Setting and clearing tab stops is not implemented beyond fixed-width tab expansion. |
| `charset-designation-and-locking-shifts` | <code>\u001b(B</code>, <code>\u001b(0</code>, <code>\u000e</code>, <code>\u000f</code> | <code>charset-designation-and-locking-shifts</code>: Character-set designation escapes, line-drawing alternate charsets, and locking shifts are not modeled yet. |
| `c1-eight-bit-controls` | <code>\u009b31m</code>, <code>\u009b12G</code> | <code>c1-eight-bit-controls</code>: 8-bit C1 control variants are not accepted as first-class parser input yet. |

## Local Verification

```bash
yarn report:ansi-display
yarn check:ansi-display-report
yarn test:conformance
yarn test:e2e:ansi-display
yarn test:e2e:tty
```
